title: Fix model frontier Lua table normalization
description: model_performance_frontier fails in plan stage with 'dictionary update sequence element #0 has length 1; 2 is required', likely from Lua table conversion into Python frontier planning. Normalize Tactus/Lua table inputs before variant planning.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Fixed plan-stage candidate_matrix normalization.

Root cause: candidate_matrix can arrive from CLI `-s` as a JSON string. The helper called `dict(candidate_matrix)`, which raises `dictionary update sequence element #0 has length 1; 2 is required` for strings.

Change:
- Added `_plain_value()` normalization in `model_performance_frontier.py` to parse JSON object/array strings and normalize Lua-style numeric tables to lists.
- Applied normalization to candidate_matrix, models, parameter_sets, each candidate object, and extra_overrides.
- Added regression test for JSON string candidate_matrix.

Validation:
- `pytest -q plexus/cli/procedure/test_model_performance_frontier.py MCP/tools/tactus_runtime/test_model_frontier_runtime.py` -> 12 passed
- Direct Project Type AI reproduction with JSON string candidate_matrix -> 2 variants: current and gpt-5.4-nano, base_model_name synced, verbosity medium
- py_compile -> passed
